speaker: THURIO
text: Sir Proteus, what says Sylvia to my suit?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: O sir, I find her milder than she was,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: And yet she takes exceptions at your person.

speaker: THURIO
text: What? That my leg is too long?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: No, that it is too little.

speaker: THURIO
text: I’ll wear a boot to make it somewhat rounder.

speaker: JULIA
text: But love will not be spurred to what it loathes.

speaker: THURIO
text: What says she to my face?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: She says it is a fair one.

speaker: THURIO
text: Nay, then the wanton lies; my face is black.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: But pearls are fair, and the old saying is,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Black men are pearls in beauteous ladies’ eyes.

speaker: JULIA
text: ’Tis true, such pearls as put out ladies’ eyes,

speaker: JULIA
text: For I had rather wink than look on them.

speaker: THURIO
text: How likes she my discourse?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Ill, when you talk of war.

speaker: THURIO
text: But well when I discourse of love and peace.

speaker: JULIA
text: But better, indeed, when you hold your peace.

speaker: THURIO
text: What says she to my valor?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: O, sir, she makes no doubt of that.

speaker: JULIA
text: She needs not when she knows it cowardice.

speaker: THURIO
text: What says she to my birth?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: That you are well derived.

speaker: JULIA
text: True, from a gentleman to a fool.

speaker: THURIO
text: Considers she my possessions?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: O, ay, and pities them.

speaker: THURIO
text: Wherefore?

speaker: JULIA
text: That such an ass should owe them.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: That they are out by lease.

speaker: JULIA
text: Here comes the Duke.

speaker: DUKE
text: How now, Sir Proteus?—How now, Thurio?

speaker: DUKE
text: Which of you saw Eglamour of late?

speaker: THURIO
text: Not I.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Nor I.

speaker: DUKE
text: Saw you my daughter?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Neither.

speaker: DUKE
text: Why, then, she’s fled unto that peasant, Valentine,

speaker: DUKE
text: And Eglamour is in her company.

speaker: DUKE
text: ’Tis true, for Friar Lawrence met them both

speaker: DUKE
text: As he, in penance, wandered through the forest;

speaker: DUKE
text: Him he knew well and guessed that it was she,

speaker: DUKE
text: But, being masked, he was not sure of it.

speaker: DUKE
text: Besides, she did intend confession

speaker: DUKE
text: At Patrick’s cell this even, and there she was not.

speaker: DUKE
text: These likelihoods confirm her flight from hence.

speaker: DUKE
text: Therefore I pray you stand not to discourse,

speaker: DUKE
text: But mount you presently and meet with me

speaker: DUKE
text: Upon the rising of the mountain foot

speaker: DUKE
text: That leads toward Mantua, whither they are fled.

speaker: DUKE
text: Dispatch, sweet gentlemen, and follow me.

speaker: THURIO
text: Why, this it is to be a peevish girl

speaker: THURIO
text: That flies her fortune when it follows her.

speaker: THURIO
text: I’ll after, more to be revenged on Eglamour

speaker: THURIO
text: Than for the love of reckless Sylvia.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: And I will follow, more for Sylvia’s love

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Than hate of Eglamour that goes with her.

speaker: JULIA
text: And I will follow, more to cross that love

speaker: JULIA
text: Than hate for Sylvia, that is gone for love.
